results.possibleIdentity: J.P. Grouen (likely a variant spelling of J.P. Groen or Jan Groen) was a technical professional and engineer active in the late 1960s and early 1970s. He was employed by Philips (specifically North American Philips or Philips Electronic Instruments) and based in Mount Vernon, New York. His primary technical field was X-ray technology, including X-ray diffraction and spectrometry. Grouen was a registered participant in the technical help program of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization founded in 1967 to facilitate collaborations between artists and engineers. In his professional capacity at Philips Electronic Instruments, he served as a product manager or engineer specialized in X-ray analytical instrumentation.
